editableGoldMemories.label: States_Experiences/Past_Experience
editableGoldMemories.evidence_text: Hello! My name is Alia, and I am a first-year medical student with 2 years of experience tutoring for the MCAT. I personally scored a 527 on the MCAT by focusing on developing efficient test taking and passage analysis strategies. Whether you are struggling with a particular concept, or need help with test-taking strategies and understanding AAMC logic, I’m here to help! Together, we can help you achieve the MCAT score of your dreams.

editableNewDialogue.text: Can you reformat a 4-week running plan into a simple table with columns for Day, Workout, and Miles?

Yes. Paste the plan text (or tell me the weekly structure), and I’ll convert it into a clean table with Day, Workout, and Miles. If there are cross-training days, should I list them as 0 miles or leave the Miles cell blank?
Also, what does it mean when a plan says “easy pace” versus “steady pace”?

“Easy pace” is a conversational effort—breathing is controlled and you could speak in full sentences (roughly RPE 3–4/10). “Steady pace” is a moderate, more focused effort—still sustainable, but conversation is shorter phrases (roughly RPE 5–6/10). Neither should feel like an all-out hard workout.
If a workout says 3x1 mile at goal pace, what should the rest be?

Common options are: (1) 2–3 minutes easy jog, (2) 2–3 minutes walk, or (3) 400m easy jog. Use shorter rest if you can hold consistent splits; longer rest if goal pace is challenging. As a general rule, keep the recoveries easy enough that the next rep starts controlled, not gasping.
Random question: is the London Marathon course considered fast?

Yes—London is generally considered a fast course because it’s relatively flat, near sea level, and often has cool spring weather. Like any race, wind and temperature on the day matter a lot, but historically it’s produced many quick times.
Ok—back to the plan. Can you include a weekly total row at the bottom of each week?

Absolutely. Once you paste the plan, I’ll format it week-by-week in tables and add a “Weekly Total” row at the bottom of each week that sums the miles (with cross-training counted as 0 unless you prefer a separate column).
Can you tweak that week-by-week running plan so my long run lands on Saturday? Sundays are basically blocked off for me.

Absolutely—I'll shift the schedule so the longest run is on Saturdays and keep Sunday as a rest/recovery day. Do you want the weekday runs earlier in the week (Mon–Wed) or spread out (Mon/Wed/Fri) to help you recover between sessions?
Quick correction for that bio detail: I’m actually originally from Oregon, not Washington. Can you swap that line so it reads correctly?
Absolutely—good catch. I’ll update it to: “Originally from Oregon, I’ve always been driven to help others, and I believe in giving back to my community in any way that I can.”
